role_delineation_check: The Social Worker is responsible for the clinical discharge plan and the logistical coordination of medical transport, whereas the Navigator focuses on verifying patient data in Atlantis, educating the family on the Healing Partners program, and flagging transition barriers to the facility team.
narrative_summary: Eleanor Vance, an 83-year-old recovering from a complex post-ICU pneumonia course, was medically cleared for home transition but faced a significant setback when her specialized medical transport failed to arrive on the scheduled morning. This logistical breakdown caused visible distress for Eleanor and her daughter, who were prepared for a noon arrival and the subsequent start of home health services. The Navigator acted as a stabilizing liaison by documenting the family's concerns in Atlantis and alerting the Social Worker to the transport gap, ensuring the transition plan was adjusted once the facility team secured a new transport window.
demographic_audit_note: Verified the daughter’s cell phone as the primary contact and corrected a transposed digit in the home zip code on the facility face sheet to ensure the Medical Assistant’s travel distance was calculated correctly in the system.
v_card_flyer_status: The Navigator inserted the Healing Partners V-Card into the daughter’s phone and reviewed the program flyer together so she would recognize the caller ID for the 24-hour follow-up call after Eleanor reaches home.
pre_dc_pulse_call: Reached the daughter 24 hours prior to the original discharge time; she expressed high readiness but noted significant anxiety about Eleanor’s mobility and breathing during the ambulance ride home.
atlantis_final_sync: Logged the revised discharge date and the confirmed Medical Assistant visit for Friday morning once the Social Worker provided verbal confirmation that the transport issue was resolved.

format_1_state_log.event_description: The Social Worker notified the Navigator that the referral was accepted by the agency and the start of care was set for Wednesday afternoon; the Navigator documented this in Atlantis and met with the family to discuss the first-day expectations.

The patient's daughter informed the Navigator that the scheduled wheelchair van never arrived and the facility staff were unsure of the delay; the Navigator documented the transport failure in Atlantis and alerted the Social Worker to the family's rising frustration.

The Social Worker provided an update that a new transport provider was secured for Thursday morning; the Navigator updated the timeline in Atlantis and conducted a brief follow-up with the family to confirm they were still comfortable with the Friday morning Medical Assistant visit.

format_2_triples.situation: The specialized transport for a post-ICU patient failed to appear at the facility.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator alerted the Social Worker to the family's confusion and documented the transport gap in Atlantis for internal tracking.

format_2_triples.situation: The daughter expressed concern about recognizing calls from the care team during the transition.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator verified that the V-Card was saved in the daughter's phone and explained its use for Caller ID recognition.

format_2_triples.situation: The family asked for the specific time the home health nurse would arrive to check Eleanor's lungs.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator deferred the clinical scheduling question to the Social Worker and the home health agency's intake team.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator noted the lack of an update regarding the rescheduled transport in Atlantis and waited for the Social Worker to provide the new timing, documenting the communication gap for the record.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the Social Worker's ownership of the discharge logistics and avoids interference with vendor management.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator asked the Social Worker for a verbal status update on the new transport window and then met with the daughter to reinforce the role of the Medical Assistant during the first day at home.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This maintains alignment with the facility team while fulfilling the Navigator's role of family education and demographic monitoring.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator contacted the medical transport dispatch office directly to find out why the van was delayed and attempted to negotiate a priority pickup for the patient to keep the transition on schedule.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This violates the no-vendor rule and oversteps into the Social Worker's responsibility for coordinating discharge logistics.
